utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format contains 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final plan includes all five top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified with costs: 1) health_coverage: specifies physical and mental health with individual monthly costs and totals monthly/annual—meets requirement. 2) property_coverage: includes home/contents (home coverage and high value items) with costs and totals monthly/annual—meets requirement. 3) travel_coverage: includes travel insurance with cost and totals monthly/annual—meets requirement. 4) additional_services: includes enhancement services with costs and totals monthly/annual—meets requirement. 5) total_budget: provides complete monthly/annual cost breakdown—meets requirement. Therefore, 5/5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: Match each named plan/add-on in the final coverage plan to the corresponding entry in the ground-truth ratings. For physical health: 'PrimeCare Premium Physical Health' maps to Primary Health Coverage > physical_health > PrimeCare Premium Plan (rating 10). For mental health: 'PrimeCare Premium Mental Health' maps to Primary Health Coverage > mental_health > PrimeCare Premium Plan (rating 10). The add-on 'Emergency Mental Health Care' exists under Health Enhancement Services with rating 8. For property: 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage' maps to Property Protection > home_content > PrimeHome Premium Coverage (rating 10). 'Premium Item Coverage' for valuables aligns with the same PrimeHome Premium Coverage umbrella; there is no separate 'Premium Item Coverage' entry in ratings, so only PrimeHome is mapped. For travel: 'PrimeTravel Premium' maps to Mobility Coverage > travel_insurance > PrimeTravel Premium Plan (rating 10). For additional services: 'InsurePlus Standard Health' maps to Primary Health Coverage > physical_health > InsurePlus Standard Plan (rating 6). 'Adventure Travel Coverage' maps to Travel Enhancement Services > Adventure Travel Coverage (rating 6). 'LifeFirst Standard Health' maps to Primary Health Coverage > physical_health > LifeFirst Standard Plan (rating 8). 'InsurePlus Standard Health' for Maria again maps to the same InsurePlus Standard Plan (rating 6).
